title: Incorrectly Implemented Greedy Coin Change Problem

statement.markdown: Given an array of coin values and a target amount, determine the minimum number of coins required to reach the target amount using greedy coin change algorithm.
statement.inputFormat: Coin values array and target amount (e.g. [1, 2, 5], 11)
statement.notes: Time complexity: O(n), where n is the number of coins

editorial.title: Greedy Coin Change Problem
editorial.intuition: Sort coins by value in descending order and use each coin to reach the target
editorial.bruteForce: Try all possible combinations of coins
editorial.optimalSolution: Sort coins by value in descending order and use each coin to reach the target
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The greedy algorithm is correct because it always uses the largest possible coin
commonMistakes: Not using the largest possible coin

functionSignature.cpp: int coinChange(int* coins, int coinsSize, int targetAmount);
functionSignature.java: int coinChange(int[] coins, int coinsSize, int targetAmount);
functionSignature.python: def coinChange(self, coins: list[int], targetAmount: int) -> int:
